name: Wahb ibn Habab
title: The Christian Youth
shortDescription: A newlywed Christian youth who, upon seeing the righteousness of Imam Hussain (AS), converted to Islam with his mother and wife.
fullStory:
Wahb ibn Habab al-Kalbi (in some sources Wahb ibn Abdullah) was a newlywed Christian youth who had pitched a tent in the desert near Tha'labiya with his mother (Qamar) and his new bride (Haniya). Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan passed by there on the way to Kufa. The Imam's miraculous and kind interaction with this Christian family (making a spring of water gush forth for them) had such a profound effect that all three converted to Islam at the hands of the Imam and eagerly joined the Hussaini caravan.

On the day of Ashura, even though Wahb had only been married for a few days, his mother encouraged him to go and assist the son of the Prophet. His mother told him: 'O my son, rise and help the son of your Prophet's daughter'. Wahb bravely went to the battlefield and, after a remarkable fight, returned to his mother and wife, asking: 'O mother, are you pleased with me?' His mother replied: 'I will not be pleased with you until you are killed in the presence of Hussain (AS)'.

Wahb's new bride, fearing for his life, sought refuge with Imam Hussain (AS) and said: 'O Imam, stop Wahb from going to the battlefield'. But upon seeing the Imam's loneliness, she too was transformed and made Wahb promise not to forget her on the Day of Judgment.

Wahb returned to the battlefield and, reciting heroic poetry, killed many enemy soldiers. Finally, he was surrounded, and both his hands were severed. He was captured and taken to Umar ibn Sa'd. Umar Sa'd, due to Wahb's unparalleled bravery, ordered him to be beheaded and his head thrown towards the Imam's tents.

Wahb's brave mother picked up the bloody head of her son, kissed it, and said: 'Praise be to God who honored me with your martyrdom alongside Hussain (AS)'. She then threw the head back at the enemy forces, saying: 'We do not take back what we have given in the way of God'. This unparalleled sacrifice by the Kalbi family became one of the most beautiful manifestations of love and devotion on Ashura.
quote: (Wahb's mother:) We do not take back what we have given in the way of God.
